title: Garlicky Sizzling Russian Butter Blini
description: A traditional Russian dish slow-cooked in a claypot, perfect for those seeking a keto-friendly hangover cure. The crumbly texture and savory flavors will keep you coming back for more.
instructions: Combine yeast and warm milk in a small bowl and let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and pepper. Add in the egg and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide into 12 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten slightly into a disk shape.
In a small bowl, mix together minced garlic and softened butter. Spread a small amount of the garlic butter on each blini.
Place the blini in the preheated claypot and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and puffed.
While the blini are baking, mix together sour cream and chopped chives in a small bowl.
Once the blini are done, brush with beaten egg and sprinkle with chopped chives.
Serve the blini hot with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of chives, if desired.
